Title: Salicylic acid biosynthesis via the PAL pathway requires benzaldehyde synthase and a benzyl salicylate-specific esterase

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: PAL-mediated salicylic acid biosynthesis; benzenoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: benzoyl-CoA to salicylic acid branch via benzaldehyde/benzyl benzoate/benzyl salicylate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces benzoyl-CoA to benzaldehyde as part of the heterodimeric benzaldehyde synthase reaction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in hybrid poplar; metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/MS and GC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes salicylic acid, salicylic acid glucoside, benzyl benzoate, and benzyl salicylate accumulation; loss increases benzoic acid and benzoic acid glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR knockout of BAldSα in hybrid poplar reduced benzyl benzoate by 78% to 97% and benzyl salicylate by 56% to 83%. 2. Knockouts reduced salicylic acid by up to 69% and salicylic acid glucoside by 77% to 98%. 3. Knockouts caused strong overaccumulation of benzoic acid and benzoic acid glucoside, supporting blockage at the benzaldehyde synthase step. 4. Results link benzaldehyde synthase to PAL-derived salicylic acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological differences reported; reduced salicinoids including salicin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to enhance PAL-derived salicylic acid and defense-related benzenoid metabolism in species retaining the pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The black poplar genome also contains two beta-subunit genes Potri.001G327800 and Potri.017G067332, but only the alpha subunit was experimentally targeted here. Residual SA in knockout lines suggests possible alternate SA pathway or minor redundancy.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Nicotiana benthamiana; Populus trichocarpa; Oryza sativa; Solanum tuberosum; Glycine max; Thuja plicata
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Populus trichocarpa; Oryza sativa; Solanum tuberosum; Glycine max; Thuja plicata
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: benzyl salicylate to salicylic acid terminal conversion
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydrolyzes benzyl salicylate to release salicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant protein enzyme assay; substrate specificity assay; enzyme kinetics; VIGS in N. benthamiana with substrate feeding; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: basal expression in leaves; induced in defense-related datasets; transcripts increased after Agrobacterium infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: benzyl salicylate infiltration; Agrobacterium-induced defense response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes conversion of benzyl salicylate to salicylic acid; silencing reduces SA accumulation from benzyl salicylate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key terminal determinant supported; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant HSR203J specifically hydrolyzed benzyl salicylate and released salicylic acid, with only weak activity on benzyl benzoate and no activity on benzyl gentisate, chlorogenic acid, or salicyl salicylate. 2. VIGS reduced HSR203J transcripts by about 70% and lowered salicylic acid accumulation from infiltrated benzyl salicylate by about 40% in N. benthamiana. 3. Orthologs from poplar, soybean, potato, rice, and western redcedar all hydrolyzed benzyl salicylate with similar kinetics, showing conservation across vascular plants. 4. Brassicaceae lacked subgroup 6 orthologs, consistent with loss of this PAL-pathway step in that family.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pTV00 VIGS vector; pBINTRA6 helper vector
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering PAL-derived salicylic acid production and defense signaling in crops that possess subgroup 6 CXEs; absent in Brassicaceae so heterologous deployment may be required there
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The authors rename HSR203J as BSE. Km for N. benthamiana enzyme with benzyl salicylate was 50.76 ± 1.96 µM; Kcat 1.85 ± 0.021 s-1. Localization was not experimentally tested.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: benzyl benzoate to benzyl salicylate hydroxylation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putatively hydroxylates benzyl benzoate at the 2-position to form benzyl salicylate
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane, cytosolic side (inferred for CYPs)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: candidate identification from expression data; VIGS in N. benthamiana; Agrobacterium-induced defense treatment; substrate feeding with benzyl benzoate; RT-qPCR; SA quantification; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-induced pathogen defense response; benzyl benzoate infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes pathogen-induced conversion of benzyl benzoate to salicylic acid; silencing reduces SA accumulation from benzyl benzoate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HSR515 was identified as the only induced CYP candidate coexpressed with BSE/HSR203J and BEBT/HSR201 in elicited tobacco datasets. 2. Agrobacterium treatment induced HSR515 expression by about 20-fold in N. benthamiana leaves, and VIGS reduced this induced transcript level by about 75%. 3. Under defense induction, benzyl benzoate feeding increased salicylic acid accumulation, and HSR515 silencing significantly suppressed this response. 4. Phylogeny places HSR515 in the CYP92A clade, which is widespread in vascular plants but absent in Brassicaceae, supporting its role as a missing PAL-pathway hydroxylase candidate.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Incomplete silencing and possible redundancy with closely related CYP92A genes
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pTV00 VIGS vector; pBINTRA6 helper vector; control pHREAC-GFP for defense induction/leaf labeling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: previous microarray data from elicitor-treated leaves used for candidate identification; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for restoring or enhancing PAL-derived salicylic acid biosynthesis in non-Brassicaceae and potentially via transgenic introduction into Brassicaceae
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic assignment remains putative because no direct recombinant enzyme assay with benzyl benzoate was reported in this paper. Poplar ortholog Potri.003G066400 clusters closely and may have related function.
